answer:
Okay, so I remember the last time I got into an argument with my friend—actually, it was kind of unexpected. We’d been working together on a big group project for our senior year, and I guess stress was running pretty high for both of us. There was this moment when we disagreed on how to divide up the work; I thought it was fair the way we had it, but my friend felt like she was doing more than her share. At first, I got defensive, you know, because I really believed I was contributing enough. But eventually, I realized that her perspective was valid, and maybe I didn’t see the whole picture.

To resolve it, I decided to just talk things out honestly. I mean, I told her how I was feeling and then I listened to her side without interrupting—which isn’t always easy when emotions are involved. We ended up rearranging our tasks to make things feel more balanced for both of us. After that, things improved a lot. We even joked about it later and agreed that being transparent is crucial in any collaboration.

Honestly, the experience made our friendship stronger because we learned we could disagree and still respect each other. It taught me a lot about compromise and empathy—not just assuming I’m always right. I also found that clear communication is essential, especially when emotions get in the way. So yeah, while it was a bit tense at first, I think we both grew from it, and now I try to approach conflicts with a bit more patience and understanding.
title: Describe the last time you got into an argument with a friend. How did you resolve the argument? How did the experience impact your relationship, and what did you learn from it?
